prompt.text: The sun ___ (shine) and we ___ (have) a lot of fun.

items.hintDe: Bei the sun (= it) nimmst du was, bei we nimmst du were. Häng an beide Wörter -ing an.
items.explainDe: The sun = es, also was shining. we braucht were having. An die Grundform kommt -ing.

prompt.text: The Titanic ___ (cross) the Atlantic when it hit an iceberg.

items.hintDe: The Titanic ist wie it, also was. Die lange Handlung in der Vergangenheit bekommt was/were + -ing.
items.explainDe: Die lange Handlung (überqueren) steht als was crossing. Die kurze (hit) bleibt im Past simple.
items.note: Canonical SB Titanic sentence; iceberg glossed.

prompt.text: While the hunter ___ (look) at the animals, they ran away.

items.hintDe: the hunter ist wie he, also was + -ing für die längere Handlung.
items.explainDe: Die lange Handlung mit while braucht was looking; die kurze Handlung (ran) steht im Past simple.
items.note: SB hunter illustration, reworked to in-gate words (animals/ran away).

prompt.text: They ___ (queue) up at the entrance of the museum.

items.hintDe: they braucht were und an das Wort kommt -ing.
items.explainDe: they were queuing: were bei they, plus -ing an queue.
items.note: SB: 'They were all queuing up at the entrance of the Greenwich Museum.'

prompt.text: At 8 o'clock I ___ (walk) my dog.

items.hintDe: Bei I nimmst du was und häng -ing an walk.
items.explainDe: I was walking: bei I steht was, plus -ing.
items.note: SB grammar box answer to 'What were you doing at 8 o'clock?'

prompt.text: The detective ___ (drive) down the street and it ___ (rain).

items.hintDe: the detective und it sind beide wie he/it, also zweimal was + -ing.
items.explainDe: was driving und was raining: bei the detective und bei it steht was.

prompt.text: At 6 o'clock I ___ (have) a bath and my sister ___ (do) her homework.

items.hintDe: Zweimal was, weil I und my sister (= she) gemeint sind. Vergiss das -ing nicht.
items.explainDe: was having / was doing: I und my sister nehmen beide was + -ing.
items.note: SB cue 'At 6 o'clock I was having a shower' (bath/sister keep it in-gate).

prompt.text: Mum and Dad ___ (watch) TV in the living room.

items.hintDe: Mum and Dad sind zwei Personen (= they), also were + -ing.
items.explainDe: were watching: bei zwei Personen (they) steht were.
items.note: SB read-and-write: 'Mum and Dad were watching TV.'

prompt.text: At 8 p.m. last night, what ___ you ___ (do)?

items.hintDe: Bei you steht were, und an do kommt -ing.
items.explainDe: What were you doing: in der Frage steht were vor you, das Wort bekommt -ing.
items.note: SB/poem-page question 'What were you doing at 8 p.m. last night?'

prompt.text: Lucas ___ (not / do) anything — Amelia just lost her phone.

items.hintDe: Bei Lucas (= he) bildest du die Verneinung mit wasn't und häng -ing an.
items.explainDe: wasn't doing: was + not (wasn't) bei he, plus -ing. Nicht didn't!
items.note: Lost-phone story; common error didn't do.

prompt.text: She ___ (not / wait) for the bus. She ___ (walk) home.

items.hintDe: Erst die Verneinung mit wasn't + -ing, dann ganz normal was + -ing.
items.explainDe: wasn't waiting / was walking: she nimmt was, in der Verneinung wasn't, immer mit -ing.

prompt.text: The two boys ___ (not / look) at the thief.

items.hintDe: The two boys (= they) verneinst du mit weren't und -ing.
items.explainDe: weren't looking: bei they steht were, verneint weren't, plus -ing.

prompt.text: We ___ (not / queue) — we were inside the museum.

items.hintDe: we braucht were, verneint weren't, und an queue kommt -ing.
items.explainDe: weren't queuing: bei we steht were, verneint weren't.

prompt.text: ___ you ___ (sleep) when I phoned you?

items.hintDe: In der Frage steht Were vor you, das Wort bekommt -ing.
items.explainDe: Were you sleeping: bei you steht Were am Satzanfang, plus -ing.

prompt.text: ___ the sun ___ (shine) when you arrived?

items.hintDe: the sun ist wie it, also Was am Anfang und -ing am Wort.
items.explainDe: Was the sun shining: bei it/the sun steht Was, plus -ing.
items.note: Greenwich weather opener as a yes/no question.

prompt.text: The sun ___ when we arrived at the museum.

items.hintDe: the sun ist Einzahl (wie it). Welche Form passt?
items.explainDe: was shining: bei the sun steht was, und das Wort braucht -ing.
items.note: Distractors: wrong be-form, missing -ing, missing be.

prompt.text: My sister ___ the guitar when I came home.

items.hintDe: My sister ist wie she. Achte auf was und auf -ing.
items.explainDe: was playing: she/my sister nimmt was, plus -ing.

prompt.text: They ___ a picnic in the park at 5 o'clock.

items.hintDe: they braucht were. Und die Form mit -ing.
items.explainDe: were having: bei they steht were, plus -ing.
items.note: Distractors flawed only in this structure.

prompt.text: Welcher Satz ist richtig?

answers.text: I was having dinner when the phone rang.

distractors: I was having dinner when the phone was ringing.
I had dinner when the phone rang.
I were having dinner when the phone rang.
items.hintDe: Die lange Handlung steht mit was/were + -ing, die kurze (das Klingeln) im Past simple.
items.explainDe: Richtig: was having (lange Handlung) + rang (kurze Handlung). Beide lang oder I were ist falsch.

distractorPool: I was having dinner when the phone rang.
I was having dinner when the phone was ringing.
I had dinner when the phone rang.
I were having dinner when the phone rang.

items.note: Mined mc-002; German instruction prompt; each distractor breaks the long/short contrast or the be-form.

prompt.text: Welcher Satz ist richtig?

answers.text: While we were eating, the lights went out.

distractors: While we eating, the lights went out.
While we were eating, the lights were going out.
While we was eating, the lights went out.
items.hintDe: Nach while steht die lange Handlung (were + -ing), die kurze (went out) im Past simple.
items.explainDe: were eating + went out ist richtig. Ohne were, mit was, oder mit were going out ist es falsch.

distractorPool: While we were eating, the lights went out.
While we eating, the lights went out.
While we were eating, the lights were going out.
While we was eating, the lights went out.

prompt.text: Gestern um 8 Uhr abends — welcher Satz beschreibt, was gerade passierte?

distractors: My sister cooks dinner every night.
items.hintDe: Du beschreibst eine Handlung, die zu einer Uhrzeit gerade ablief. Welche Form zeigt das?
items.explainDe: was cooking zeigt: die Handlung war zu 8 Uhr im Gange. Die anderen Formen passen nicht zu 'gerade gestern um 8'.

distractorPool: My sister cooks dinner every night.

prompt.text: Du erzählst von dem Moment, als der Feueralarm in der Schule losging. Welcher Satz passt?

answers.text: Our teacher was reading a story when the alarm went off.

distractors: Our teacher read a story when the alarm went off.
Our teacher reads a story when the alarm goes off.
Our teacher was reading a story when the alarm was going off.
items.hintDe: Die lange Handlung (lesen) läuft, als die kurze (Alarm) sie unterbricht.
items.explainDe: was reading + went off ist richtig: lange Handlung in der Verlaufsform, kurze im Past simple.

distractorPool: Our teacher was reading a story when the alarm went off.
Our teacher read a story when the alarm went off.
Our teacher reads a story when the alarm goes off.
Our teacher was reading a story when the alarm was going off.

items.note: Mined cp-020; reworked to in-gate verb (read a story).

prompt.text: Ich ging gerade zur Schule, als es zu regnen begann.

answers.text: I was walking to school when it began to rain.
I was walking to school when it began raining.

items.hintDe: 'ging gerade' = was walking (lange Handlung). 'begann' bleibt im Past simple (began).
items.explainDe: I was walking ... when it began: die lange Handlung in der Verlaufsform, die kurze (began) im Past simple.
items.note: Mined tr-001; 'began' is the in-gate interrupting verb.

prompt.text: Während wir zu Abend aßen, klingelte plötzlich das Telefon.

answers.text: While we were having dinner, the phone suddenly rang.
While we were eating dinner, the phone suddenly rang.
While we were having dinner, the phone rang suddenly.

items.hintDe: Nach 'während' steht die lange Handlung mit were + -ing, 'klingelte' im Past simple.
items.explainDe: While we were having dinner, the phone rang: lange Handlung in der Verlaufsform, kurze im Past simple.

prompt.text: Was hast du gestern um 9 Uhr gemacht?

answers.text: What were you doing at 9 o'clock yesterday?
What were you doing yesterday at 9 o'clock?

items.hintDe: 'Was hast du gerade gemacht?' = What were you doing? Bei you steht were.
items.explainDe: What were you doing: bei you steht were, das Wort bekommt -ing.

prompt.text: Die Sonne schien und alle stellten sich am Eingang an.

answers.text: The sun was shining and everybody was queuing at the entrance.
The sun was shining and everybody was queueing at the entrance.

items.hintDe: Beide Handlungen liefen gleichzeitig ab: was shining und was queuing.
items.explainDe: was shining / was queuing: beide langen Handlungen stehen in der Verlaufsform.

prompt.text: Ich habe gerade ein Buch gelesen, als du mich angerufen hast.

answers.text: I was reading a book when you called me.
I was reading a book when you phoned me.
I was reading a book when you rang me.

items.hintDe: 'las gerade' = was reading (lange Handlung), 'riefst an' im Past simple.
items.explainDe: I was reading ... when you called: lange Handlung in der Verlaufsform, kurze im Past simple.

prompt.text: I were playing football when Mum called.

answers.text: I was playing football when Mum called.

items.hintDe: Bei I heißt es nicht were. Welche Form von be passt?
items.explainDe: Bei I steht was, nicht were: I was playing.

prompt.text: She was walk to the shop when she met her friend.

answers.text: She was walking to the shop when she met her friend.

items.hintDe: Nach was fehlt eine Endung am Wort.
items.explainDe: Nach was kommt die Form mit -ing: was walking.

prompt.text: I was walking to school when I was seeing a dog.

answers.text: I was walking to school when I saw a dog.

items.hintDe: Die kurze Handlung (den Hund sehen) darf nicht in der Verlaufsform stehen.
items.explainDe: Die kurze, unterbrechende Handlung steht im Past simple: when I saw a dog.
items.note: Classic error: short action wrongly in continuous.

prompt.text: They was having a picnic in the park.

answers.text: They were having a picnic in the park.

items.hintDe: they passt nicht zu was.
items.explainDe: Bei they steht were: They were having.

prompt.text: She was reading a magazine while the dog was siting on the sofa.

answers.text: She was reading a magazine while the dog was sitting on the sofa.

items.hintDe: Ein Wort ist falsch geschrieben: sit verdoppelt vor -ing den letzten Buchstaben.
items.explainDe: sit wird zu sitting (Doppel-t): was sitting.

prompt.text: The thief was watch the two boys and the woman.

answers.text: The thief was watching the two boys and the woman.

items.hintDe: Nach was fehlt das -ing.
items.explainDe: was watching: nach was kommt die Form mit -ing.

prompt.text: The phone rang. We were having dinner. (join with WHILE)

answers.text: While we were having dinner, the phone rang.
The phone rang while we were having dinner.

items.hintDe: Die lange Handlung kommt nach while mit were + -ing, die kurze bleibt im Past simple.
items.explainDe: While we were having dinner, the phone rang: lange Handlung mit while, kurze im Past simple.

prompt.text: I walk my dog. (8 o'clock last night)

answers.text: I was walking my dog at 8 o'clock last night.
At 8 o'clock last night I was walking my dog.

items.hintDe: Mache aus 'I walk' eine Handlung, die um 8 Uhr gerade ablief.
items.explainDe: I was walking my dog: bei I steht was, plus -ing.
items.note: Present -> past continuous at a clock time.

prompt.text: She reads a book. The phone rings. (join with WHILE)

answers.text: While she was reading a book, the phone rang.
The phone rang while she was reading a book.

items.hintDe: Lange Handlung nach while mit was + -ing, kurze (rang) im Past simple.
items.explainDe: While she was reading a book, the phone rang: lange Handlung in der Verlaufsform, kurze im Past simple.

prompt.text: He looks at his phone. He walks into a lamp post. (join with WHILE)

answers.text: While he was looking at his phone, he walked into a lamp post.
He walked into a lamp post while he was looking at his phone.

items.hintDe: Die lange Handlung (aufs Handy schauen) läuft, als die kurze (walk into) passiert.
items.explainDe: While he was looking at his phone, he walked into a lamp post: lange Handlung mit while, kurze im Past simple.
items.note: WB ex.7 Billy idea; 'walked into' keeps it in-gate (crash avoided).

prompt.text: you / sleep / when I phoned you

answers.text: Were you sleeping when I phoned you?
Were you sleeping when I called you?

items.hintDe: Beginne die Frage mit Were, dann you, dann das Wort mit -ing.
items.explainDe: Were you sleeping ...?: in der Frage steht Were vor you, plus -ing.

prompt.text: what / your sister / do / at 6 p.m.

answers.text: What was your sister doing at 6 p.m.?

items.hintDe: your sister ist wie she, also Was. Frage: What was she doing ...?
items.explainDe: What was your sister doing: bei she/your sister steht was, das Wort bekommt -ing.

prompt.text: she / was / cooking / dinner / at / 6 / pm

answers.text: She was cooking dinner at 6 pm.
At 6 pm she was cooking dinner.

items.hintDe: Reihenfolge: she + was + Wort mit -ing + Rest.
items.explainDe: She was cooking dinner at 6 pm: was bei she, plus -ing.

distractorPool: She was cooking dinner at 6 pm.

prompt.text: she / was / doing / her / homework / when / the / phone / rang

answers.text: She was doing her homework when the phone rang.

items.hintDe: Lange Handlung: was doing. Kurze Handlung (phone): rang im Past simple.
items.explainDe: She was doing her homework when the phone rang: lange Handlung mit was + -ing, kurze im Past simple.

distractorPool: She was doing her homework when the phone rang.

prompt.text: the / sun / was / shining / and / we / were / having / fun

answers.text: The sun was shining and we were having fun.

items.hintDe: the sun (= it) nimmt was shining; we nimmt were having.
items.explainDe: The sun was shining and we were having fun: was bei the sun, were bei we, beide mit -ing.

distractorPool: The sun was shining and we were having fun.

items.note: Verbatim SB scene-setting line as a build.

prompt.text: Anfang und passendes Ende

pairs.left: The Titanic was crossing the Atlantic

pairs.right: we were having a lot of fun.

items.hintDe: Die lange Handlung (was/were + -ing) trifft auf die kurze Handlung im Past simple oder auf einen zweiten Hintergrund.
items.explainDe: Jeder Anfang in der Verlaufsform passt zu genau einer kurzen Handlung (Past simple) oder zu einem zweiten langen Teil.
items.note: Unique right-sides; SB carriers; iceberg glossed.

prompt.text: Frage und passende Antwort

pairs.left: What were you doing at 8 p.m.?

pairs.right: He was driving down the street.

items.hintDe: Achte darauf, ob die Frage mit Was oder Were beginnt und ob eine W-Frage eine ganze Antwort braucht.
items.explainDe: W-Fragen (What ...?) brauchen eine ganze Antwort, Ja/Nein-Fragen die Kurzantwort mit was/were.
items.note: Unique right-sides; mix of Was/Were and W/yes-no.

prompt.text: Welche Form passt? Sortiere die Personen.

items.hintDe: was steht bei I/he/she/it (und Einzahl-Namen), were bei you/we/they (und Mehrzahl).
items.explainDe: I/she/he/the dog nehmen was; you/we/they/the boys nehmen were.
items.note: Sort subjects by was vs were.

prompt.text: Lange oder kurze Handlung? Sortiere die Satzteile.

groups.label: was crossing
members: The Titanic was crossing the Atlantic

items.hintDe: Die lange Handlung steht mit was/were + -ing, die kurze im Past simple.
items.explainDe: was crossing/were having sind lange Handlungen; hit/rang/saw sind kurze Handlungen im Past simple.

prompt.text: Richtig oder falsch? Sortiere die Sätze.

items.hintDe: Achte auf was/were und auf das -ing am Wort.
items.explainDe: Richtig: I was, they were, was doing. Falsch sind I were, they was und was do (ohne -ing).
items.note: Each wrong member breaks only be-form or -ing.

prompt.text: Die Form von 'sit', die nach was/were kommt:

items.hintDe: Nach was/were kommt die Form mit -ing. Bei sit wird das t verdoppelt.
items.explainDe: sit + ting = sitting: der letzte Buchstabe wird vor -ing verdoppelt.

prompt.text: Die Form von 'run', die nach was/were kommt:

items.hintDe: Nach was/were steht die Form mit -ing. Bei run wird das n verdoppelt.
items.explainDe: run + ning = running: der letzte Buchstabe wird verdoppelt.

prompt.text: Die Form von 'cross' im Satz 'The Titanic was ___ the Atlantic.':

items.hintDe: Nach was kommt die Form mit -ing.
items.explainDe: cross + ing = crossing: was crossing.
items.note: Single token tied to Titanic carrier.

prompt.text: Welche Form von be passt zur Person?

items.hintDe: was/wasn't bei I/he/she/it, were/weren't bei you/we/they.
items.explainDe: I was, they were, she wasn't, we weren't: was bei Einzahl, were bei Mehrzahl.
